records.ety: [OE. breth, breeth, AS. brǣð odor, scent, breath; cf. OHG. brādam steam, vapor, breath, G. brodem, and possibly E. Brawn, and Breed.]

defs: The air inhaled and exhaled in respiration; air which, in the process of respiration, has parted with oxygen and has received carbonic acid, aqueous vapor, warmth, etc.
quotes: Melted as breath into the wind.

defs: The act of breathing naturally or freely; the power or capacity to breathe freely; as, I am out of breath.
The power of respiration, and hence, life.
quotes: Thou takest away their breath, they die.
Give me some breath, some little pause.

defs: A single respiration, or the time of making it; a single act; an instant.
quotes: He smiles and he frowns in a breath.

defs: Fig.: That which gives or strengthens life.
quotes: The earthquake voice of victory, To thee the breath of life.

defs: A single word; the slightest effort; a trifle.
quotes: A breath can make them, as a breath has made.

defs: A very slight breeze; air in gentle motion.
quotes: Calm and unruffled as a summer's sea, when not a breath of wind flies o'er its surface.

defs: Gentle exercise, causing a quicker respiration.
